# Performance Test Scenarios - Cart Icon Feature

**Generated By**: GitHub Copilot  
**Date**: January 28, 2026  
**Total Scenarios**: 15  
**Test Type**: Performance  

## Performance Requirements
- **NFR-1**: Badge update latency <100ms
- **NFR-2**: localStorage save <50ms, load <100ms
- **Target**: 60fps animations, no memory leaks, UI remains responsive

---

## Performance Test Scenarios

| Test ID | Test Name | Benchmark | Priority | Measurement Method | Test Steps | Expected Results | Pass/Warning/Fail Criteria |
|---------|-----------|-----------|----------|-------------------|------------|------------------|---------------------------|
| TS-CART-P-001 | Badge Update Time After addItem() | <100ms | Critical | Chrome DevTools Performance tab, React Profiler | 1. Open Chrome DevTools Performance tab<br>2. Start recording<br>3. Add item to cart from Products page<br>4. Stop recording after badge updates<br>5. Analyze render timing | Badge updates within 100ms of addItem() call, no frame drops | Pass: <100ms<br>Warning: 100-150ms<br>Fail: >150ms |
| TS-CART-P-002 | Badge Update Time After removeItem() | <100ms | Critical | Chrome DevTools Performance tab, React Profiler | 1. Start with cart containing 5 items<br>2. Open Performance tab, start recording<br>3. Remove one item from cart<br>4. Stop recording after badge updates<br>5. Measure render time | Badge updates within 100ms, UI remains responsive | Pass: <100ms<br>Warning: 100-150ms<br>Fail: >150ms |
| TS-CART-P-003 | Badge Update With Large Cart (50+ items) | <100ms | High | React Profiler, manual timing with Performance API | 1. Load cart with 50+ items<br>2. Use Performance.now() before addItem()<br>3. Add new item to cart<br>4. Use Performance.now() after badge re-renders<br>5. Calculate elapsed time | Badge updates within 100ms even with large cart data | Pass: <100ms<br>Warning: 100-150ms<br>Fail: >150ms |
| TS-CART-P-004 | Rendering Performance With 100+ Items | No UI blocking | High | Chrome DevTools Performance tab, FPS meter | 1. Load cart with 100+ items<br>2. Enable FPS meter in DevTools<br>3. Add/remove items from cart<br>4. Observe FPS and main thread blocking<br>5. Check for janky animations | UI remains responsive, FPS stays above 30, no long tasks >50ms | Pass: FPS >30, no blocking<br>Warning: FPS 20-30<br>Fail: FPS <20, UI frozen |
| TS-CART-P-005 | No UI Blocking During State Updates | Main thread free | Critical | Chrome DevTools Performance tab, Long Tasks API | 1. Open Performance tab with CPU throttling (4x slowdown)<br>2. Perform rapid cart operations (add 10 items quickly)<br>3. Check for long tasks (>50ms blocking)<br>4. Verify UI remains interactive | No long tasks >50ms, user can still interact with UI during updates | Pass: No tasks >50ms<br>Warning: 1-2 tasks 50-100ms<br>Fail: Tasks >100ms or UI frozen |
| TS-CART-P-006 | localStorage Save Time After Cart Change | <50ms | Critical | Performance API (performance.now()) | 1. Wrap saveCartToStorage() with performance.now()<br>2. Add item to cart<br>3. Measure time from start of save to completion<br>4. Repeat 10 times, calculate average | localStorage save completes in <50ms on average | Pass: <50ms avg<br>Warning: 50-100ms avg<br>Fail: >100ms avg |
| TS-CART-P-007 | localStorage Load Time On Page Refresh | <100ms | Critical | Performance API, Chrome DevTools Application tab | 1. Load cart with 20 items<br>2. Add performance timing to loadCartFromStorage()<br>3. Refresh page<br>4. Measure time from localStorage.getItem() to cart state initialized<br>5. Check Performance tab for "Parse JSON" timing | Cart loaded from localStorage in <100ms | Pass: <100ms<br>Warning: 100-200ms<br>Fail: >200ms |
| TS-CART-P-008 | localStorage Operations With Large Cart (>5KB) | <100ms read/write | High | Chrome DevTools Application tab, Performance API | 1. Load cart with 100+ items (~5-8KB JSON)<br>2. Check localStorage size in Application tab<br>3. Measure save time with Performance API<br>4. Refresh and measure load time<br>5. Verify no performance degradation | Save <50ms, load <100ms even with large data | Pass: Within benchmarks<br>Warning: +50% over benchmark<br>Fail: >2x benchmark |
| TS-CART-P-009 | localStorage Quota Management | <5MB total usage | Medium | Chrome DevTools Application tab | 1. Check current localStorage usage<br>2. Add items until cart reaches ~1MB JSON size<br>3. Verify quota not exceeded (typical limit 5-10MB)<br>4. Check for quota errors in console<br>5. Test save/load still works | Cart operations work, no quota exceeded errors, usage <5MB | Pass: <5MB, no errors<br>Warning: 5-8MB, still works<br>Fail: Quota error or >10MB |
| TS-CART-P-010 | Cart Icon Interactive Within 100ms After Page Load | <100ms Time to Interactive | Critical | Lighthouse Performance audit, Chrome DevTools | 1. Clear cache, hard reload page<br>2. Run Lighthouse performance audit<br>3. Check "Time to Interactive" metric<br>4. Manually test cart icon click immediately after page visible<br>5. Verify icon responsive | Cart icon clickable and responsive within 100ms of page becoming interactive | Pass: <100ms TTI for icon<br>Warning: 100-200ms<br>Fail: >200ms or not clickable |
| TS-CART-P-011 | Toast Notification Appears Within 200ms | <200ms | High | Performance API, manual observation | 1. Wrap toast.success() with performance timing<br>2. Add item to cart<br>3. Measure time from cart operation to toast visible in DOM<br>4. Repeat 5 times, calculate average | Toast appears within 200ms of cart action | Pass: <200ms avg<br>Warning: 200-300ms avg<br>Fail: >300ms avg |
| TS-CART-P-012 | Badge Animation Smooth (60fps) | 60fps, no jank | Medium | Chrome DevTools FPS meter, Performance tab | 1. Enable FPS meter in DevTools<br>2. Add/remove items to trigger badge animations<br>3. Record FPS during animations<br>4. Check Performance tab for dropped frames<br>5. Verify CSS transitions smooth | Badge animations render at 60fps, no dropped frames | Pass: 55-60fps consistently<br>Warning: 45-55fps<br>Fail: <45fps or janky |
| TS-CART-P-013 | No Memory Leaks After 100+ Operations | <5MB memory growth | High | Chrome DevTools Memory tab, heap snapshots | 1. Take initial heap snapshot<br>2. Perform 100 cart operations (add/remove items)<br>3. Force garbage collection (DevTools > Memory > Collect garbage)<br>4. Take second heap snapshot<br>5. Compare memory delta | Memory growth <5MB after 100 operations, no detached DOM nodes | Pass: <5MB growth<br>Warning: 5-10MB growth<br>Fail: >10MB or memory leak detected |
| TS-CART-P-014 | CartContext Cleanup On Unmount | No lingering subscriptions | Medium | React DevTools Profiler, Chrome Memory tab | 1. Navigate to Products page (CartContext mounted)<br>2. Take heap snapshot<br>3. Navigate away (unmount CartContext)<br>4. Force garbage collection<br>5. Take second snapshot, check for detached listeners | CartContext properly cleaned up, no lingering event listeners or subscriptions | Pass: Clean unmount, no leaks<br>Warning: Minor retained objects<br>Fail: Event listeners not removed |
| TS-CART-P-015 | Toast Notifications Cleaned Up (No Stale DOM) | No stale toasts in DOM | Medium | Chrome DevTools Elements tab, inspect DOM | 1. Add 10 items to cart rapidly (trigger 10 toasts)<br>2. Wait for all toasts to auto-dismiss (3 seconds each)<br>3. Inspect DOM for toast container<br>4. Verify no stale toast elements remain<br>5. Check for memory leaks related to toasts | All dismissed toasts removed from DOM, no memory leaks | Pass: All toasts cleaned up<br>Warning: 1-2 stale elements<br>Fail: Multiple stale toasts or memory leak |

---

## Performance Testing Notes

### Tools Required
- **Chrome DevTools**:
  - Performance tab for timing analysis
  - Memory tab for heap snapshots and leak detection
  - Application tab for localStorage inspection
  - FPS meter for animation performance
- **React DevTools**: Profiler for component render timing
- **Lighthouse**: Overall performance audit
- **Performance API**: Programmatic timing measurements (Performance.now())

### Best Practices
1. Test with **CPU throttling** (4x slowdown) to simulate slower devices
2. Test with **network throttling** (not critical for localStorage, but good practice)
3. Take multiple measurements and calculate **averages** for consistency
4. **Clear cache** between test runs for accurate initial load measurements
5. Use **production build** for realistic performance (dev builds have extra overhead)

### Performance Benchmarks Summary
- **Badge Update**: <100ms (NFR-1)
- **localStorage Save**: <50ms
- **localStorage Load**: <100ms (NFR-2)
- **Time to Interactive**: <100ms for cart icon
- **Toast Display**: <200ms
- **Animations**: 60fps, smooth transitions
- **Memory Growth**: <5MB per 100 operations
- **No UI Blocking**: No tasks >50ms

### Pass/Warning/Fail Criteria
- **Pass**: Meets or exceeds benchmark
- **Warning**: Within 50% of benchmark (acceptable but could be optimized)
- **Fail**: Exceeds 2x benchmark or causes noticeable UX degradation
